pain_points: high OTA commission dependency (20-30% per booking)
low direct booking rate despite strong TripAdvisor presence
no direct booking widget on website
Book Now CTA on site but no booking system detectable behind it
poor conversion of web traffic to bookings
peak season capacity not being captured in advance
competitor booking software that lacks international distribution
positive_signals: existing booking software (easier switch than starting from scratch)
previous demo or conversation in CRM
Book Now CTA present — intent to take online bookings, even if system is missing or broken
competitor software detected on site — confirms they are open to using booking technology
negative_signals: business listed as for sale or being sold
explicit 'happy with current software' objection with no follow-up trigger
90%+ walk-in business with no online presence
no activity in CRM for 6+ months with no context

website_mismatches.signal: Book Now CTA with no booking system detectable
website_mismatches.what_it_means: They want online bookings but either have no system, a broken flow, or an undetectable redirect. High-value gap.
website_mismatches.how_to_use: Surface it gently as an observation — ask what is happening behind the button, not whether they need help.

website_mismatches.signal: No booking capability at all (no widget, no CTA)
website_mismatches.what_it_means: Business is taking bookings offline. International pre-arrival visitors have no way to book direct.
website_mismatches.how_to_use: Frame in terms of what a potential customer sees when they try to book — not as a flaw, but as a gap worth understanding.

website_mismatches.signal: Competitor software on site (Rezdy, Checkfront, Bokun, etc.)
website_mismatches.what_it_means: They use booking software. May be underserved on international distribution or direct booking share.
website_mismatches.how_to_use: Ask how it is working for them — specifically around direct vs OTA split. Do not pitch against the software directly.

website_mismatches.signal: OTA review badges prominent, no direct booking widget
website_mismatches.what_it_means: They are sending booking intent to OTAs via their own website. Every Viator click costs them 20-30% commission.
website_mismatches.how_to_use: Good supporting signal. Rarely a standalone angle — pair with a CRM signal or TripAdvisor presence.

website_mismatches.signal: CRM records one software, website shows different software
website_mismatches.what_it_means: CRM data may be stale, or they have switched systems. Worth clarifying before making assumptions.
website_mismatches.how_to_use: Note as a gap. Do not lead with it — use it to ask a clarifying question if the conversation opens.
